name: Abadar
title: The Master of the First Vault
description: Abadar is the god of cities, wealth, and orderly commerce. He represents civilization, law, and the structured acquisition of wealth through legitimate means. Abadar's followers believe in the power of commerce and law to create orderly, prosperous societies. His worship is particularly strong among merchants, traders, and urban dwellers.

name: Asmodeus
title: The Prince of Darkness
description: Asmodeus is the lord of all devils and master of contracts. He embodies tyranny, slavery, and pride, representing lawful evil at its most insidious. His worshippers seek power through cunning deals and diabolical pacts. Asmodeus is worshipped by those who believe in order through dominion and personal advancement through any means.

name: Calistria
title: The Savored Sting
description: Calistria is the goddess of trickery, seduction, and revenge. She represents the chaos of passion and the cunning of the con artist. Her followers believe in personal freedom and settling scores through wit and manipulation. She is patron to thieves, con artists, and those who have been wronged and seek payback.

name: Cayden Cailean
title: The Accidental God
description: Cayden Cailean is the god of freedom, adventure, and good fortune. An ascended mortal, he became a god through a drunken feat. He represents freedom from oppression and the joy of camaraderie. His worship appeals to those who love freedom, adventure, and celebration.

name: Desna
title: The Tender of Dreams
description: Desna is the goddess of dreams, stars, and travelers. She watches over those who journey in darkness and those who sleep. Desna represents hope, freedom, and the wonder of the unknown. She is particularly important to travelers, dreamers, and those seeking new horizons.

name: Erastil
title: The Old Deadeye
symbol: Bow and arrow with oak leaves
description: Erastil is the god of farmers, hunters, community, and family. He represents the old ways and the bonds that hold societies together. His followers value honest work, self-reliance, and community welfare. He is worshipped in rural areas and by those who depend on the land.

name: Gorum
title: The Strength of Stone
description: Gorum is the god of strength, battle, and weapons. He represents the raw power of combat and the pride of martial prowess. His followers believe in strength through physical prowess and dominance in battle. Gorum appeals to warriors, barbarians, and those who settle disputes through combat.

name: Gozreh
title: The Wind and the Waves
symbol: Wavy line over a curved line
description: Gozreh is the god of nature, weather, and the sea. Neither male nor female, this deity represents the primal forces of nature in all their wild freedom. Gozreh's followers revere nature's raw power and seek balance with the natural world. Druids and rangers particularly honor this ancient god.

name: Iomedae
title: The Goddess of Justice
description: Iomedae is the goddess of justice, valor, and righteous combat. An ascended mortal chosen by Aroden, she represents the highest ideals of chivalry and lawful good. Her followers are champions of justice and defenders of the innocent. Paladins and holy warriors particularly revere her.

name: Irori
title: The Master of Masters
description: Irori is the god of knowledge, history, and self-perfection. He teaches that perfection can be achieved through dedicated study and improvement. His followers seek to better themselves through education and discipline. Monks and scholars particularly revere this philosophical deity.

name: Lamashtu
title: The Mother of Madness
description: Lamashtu is the goddess of madness, monsters, and nightmares. She is a primal force of chaos and destruction, corruption and depravity. Her followers are typically mad, bestial, or utterly depraved. She is opposed by all good faiths and is particularly reviled by those who protect the innocent.

name: Nethys
title: The God of Magic
description: Nethys is the god of magic itself, all-seeing and unknowable. He is both helpful and dangerous, representing the duality of magic as a force of creation and destruction. His followers seek magical knowledge above all else. Wizards and sorcerers particularly venerate this mysterious deity.

name: Norgorber
title: The Reaper of Reputation
description: Norgorber is the god of greed, secrets, murder, and poison. He represents the darkest aspects of ambition and selfishness. Few openly worship him, preferring to pray in secret. His followers include assassins, poisoners, and those with dark secrets to keep.

name: Pharasma
title: The Lady of Graves
description: Pharasma is the goddess of death, fate, and prophecy. She guides the souls of the dead to the afterlife and judges them accordingly. Her followers believe in the natural cycle of life and death. Clerics and healers often revere her as a counterforce to undeath.

name: Rovagug
title: The Rough Beast
description: Rovagug is the god of wrath, destruction, and chaos. A primal force of destruction imprisoned at the world's beginning, his followers pray for apocalyptic destruction. Few openly worship him. His domain is that of natural disasters and uncontrolled fury.

name: Sarenrae
title: The Dawnflower
description: Sarenrae is the goddess of the sun, healing, and redemption. She brings warmth and light to the world. Her followers believe in redemption for those who seek it and oppose undead. She is one of the most popular deities, revered for her healing and her call to redemption.

name: Shelyn
title: The Eternal Rose
description: Shelyn is the goddess of beauty, art, love, and creativity. She inspires artists and musicians, and encourages love and appreciation for beauty. Her followers seek to create beauty and promote love in the world. She is patron to artists, musicians, and lovers.

name: Torag
title: The Father of Creation
description: Torag is the god of dwarves, craftsmanship, protection, and strategy. He is the father of creation and teaches his followers to forge both weapons and strong communities. His worship is particularly important to dwarves and those who work with metal. He represents the values of hard work and careful planning.

name: Urgathoa
title: The Pallid Plague
description: Urgathoa is the goddess of gluttony, disease, and undeath. She represents the evil desires of the body without restraint. Her followers seek eternal life through undeath and indulgence. She is opposed by all who value natural life and health.

name: Zon-Kuthon
title: The Midnight Lord
description: Zon-Kuthon is the god of pain, darkness, and suffering. An outsider drawn to the Material Plane, he has established a following of pain-worshippers. His followers seek to cause suffering or inflict pain upon themselves. He represents the darkest aspects of despair and loss.

name: Alseta
title: The Mistress of Transitions
description: Alseta is the goddess of transitions and doorways, watching over those who cross thresholds both physical and metaphorical. Her followers believe in the importance of change and new beginnings. She is less commonly worshipped but important to merchants, travelers, and those making major life changes.

name: Apsu
title: The Waybringer
description: Apsu is the god of good dragons and represents goodness, wisdom, and leadership. He is the celestial opposite of Dahak. His followers include good-aligned dragons and those who revere draconic virtues. He teaches that wisdom and protection go hand in hand.

name: Brigh
title: The Clockwork Goddess
description: Brigh is the goddess of invention, machinery, and progress through technology. She represents the mechanical and orderly side of creation. Her followers are inventors, artificers, and engineers seeking to improve the world through technological means.

name: Dahak
title: The Rending Dragon
description: Dahak is the god of evil dragons and represents chaos, destruction, and greed. He opposes Apsu and teaches that might makes right. His few followers are typically evil dragons or those corrupted by draconic evil. He embodies the worst aspects of draconic pride.

name: Ghlaunder
title: The Gossip
description: Ghlaunder is the god of parasites, infections, and pestilence. He spreads corruption and disease through insects and parasitic creatures. Few openly worship him, but some plague cultists and those corrupted by disease may appeal to him.

name: Groetus
title: The End Times
description: Groetus is the god of the end times and apocalypse. He represents the inevitable end of all things. Few openly worship him, but his followers believe in the ultimate doom that awaits the world.

name: Hanspur
title: The Drowning God
description: Hanspur is the god of rivers, water travel, and fishing. He watches over those who travel by water and controls the forces of flowing water. His followers include sailors, fishermen, and those who rely on rivers for commerce.

name: Milani
title: The Everlight
description: Milani is the goddess of hope, devotion, and uprisings against oppression. She inspires those who fight tyranny and seek freedom. Her followers believe in the power of hope to overcome despair and the right of all people to be free.

name: Sivanah
title: The Mystress of Illusions
description: Sivanah is the goddess of illusions, mysteries, and secrets. She represents the ambiguity between truth and falsehood. Her followers appreciate mystery and the hidden aspects of reality. She teaches that not everything is as it seems.

name: Achaekek
title: The Mantis God
description: Achaekek is the god of assassins and represents the perfect predator. He appears as a praying mantis and is revered by those who kill for contract or coin. His followers believe in discipline, honor among assassins, and perfect execution of contracts.

name: Besmara
title: The Pirate Queen
description: Besmara is the goddess of pirates, sea monsters, and nautical adventure. She represents the chaotic freedom of the open sea and the thrill of plunder. Her followers include pirates, sailors, and those who love freedom and adventure.

name: Kurgess
title: The Mighty One
description: Kurgess is the god of competition, sport, and athletic achievement. He encourages friendly competition and celebrates physical prowess used for good. His followers value fair play, physical fitness, and the honest pursuit of athletic excellence.

name: Naderi
title: The Temptress
description: Naderi is the goddess of romantic tragedy and drowning. She represents the bittersweet nature of love and loss. Her followers include tragic lovers and those who have lost themselves to sorrow.

name: Zyphus
title: The Black Rider
description: Zyphus is the god of accidental death, suicides, and graveyards. He represents tragic and meaningless death. His followers are a dark cult of those who dwell in graveyards or plot accidental deaths.

name: Ragathiel
title: The Angel of Vengeance
description: Ragathiel is the Empyreal Lord of chivalry, duty, and righteous vengeance. He guides those who seek to bring justice through strength. His followers are knights and warriors devoted to righteousness and the protection of the innocent.

name: Andoletta
title: Grandmother Crow
description: Andoletta is the Empyreal Lord of consolation and comfort. She watches over those who grieve and provides solace to the afflicted. Her followers include healers and those devoted to comforting others.

name: Arshea
title: Spirit of Abandon
description: Arshea is the Empyreal Lord of passion, freedom, and natural beauty. She encourages celebration and the expression of joy. Her followers believe in the beauty of life and the right to experience pleasure and freedom.

name: Black Butterfly
title: The Silence Between
description: Black Butterfly is the Empyreal Lord of distance and separation. She represents the space between things and the journeys between destinations. Her followers value independence and personal space.

name: Korada
title: The Open Hand
description: Korada is the Empyreal Lord of peace and nonviolence. He teaches that conflict can be avoided through understanding and that inner peace comes through meditation and discipline. His followers are pacifists and those devoted to conflict resolution.

name: Pulura
title: The Shimmering Maiden
description: Pulura is the Empyreal Lord of stars and constellations. She guides travelers by the night sky and speaks through dreams and visions. Her followers are stargazers and dreamers devoted to following their destinies.

name: Sinashakti
title: The Immaculate Joy
description: Sinashakti is the Empyreal Lord of journeys and wandering. She represents the joy found in travel and discovery. Her followers are adventurers and travelers devoted to exploring the world and finding happiness in movement.

name: Vildeis
title: The Cardinal Martyr
description: Vildeis is the Empyreal Lord of martyrdom and devoted faith. She inspires those who sacrifice themselves for the greater good. Her followers are devoted to their ideals and willing to suffer for their beliefs.

name: Barbatos
title: The Hoarder
description: Barbatos is one of the great Archdevils, lord of Avernus and a master of hoarding. He rules through tyranny and accumulation of power. Few mortals dare to worship him directly.

name: Dispater
title: The Iron Judge
description: Dispater is the Archdevil of Dis and represents lawful, orderly evil. He rules through iron discipline and absolute law. His domain is that of hierarchies and rigid order imposed through force.

name: Mammon
title: The Treasure Lord
description: Mammon is the Archdevil of Erebus and represents greed and material wealth. He collects wealth and power through corrupt commerce. His followers seek material gain above all else.

name: Belial
title: The Deceiver
description: Belial is the Archdevil of Phlegethon and represents deception and cruel corruption. He deceives mortals into contracts and corruption. His followers are those who have been corrupted or seek to corrupt others.

name: Geryon
title: The Shackler
description: Geryon is the Archdevil of Stygia and represents slavery and betrayal. He rules through chains and enslavement. His followers are slavers and those who profit from the suffering of others.

name: Mephistopheles
title: The Contracts of Hell
description: Mephistopheles is the Archdevil of Caina and the master of infernal contracts. He represents cunning and the power of agreement. His followers are those who have made Faustian bargains with the devil.

name: Abraxas
title: The Forgotten God
description: Abraxas is a powerful Demon Lord who represents forbidden knowledge and dangerous magical secrets. He is often worshipped by those seeking forbidden arcane power. His followers include mad wizards and those seeking knowledge at any cost.

name: Baphomet
title: The Horned Beast
description: Baphomet is the Demon Lord of beasts and labyrinths. He represents the corruption of civilization into barbarism. His followers include savage cultists and those who have abandoned their humanity.

name: Deskari
title: The Locust That Devours
description: Deskari is the Demon Lord of the Worldwound and represents destruction through plague and corruption. His armies of locusts and demons devastate the land. He is perhaps the most directly threatening demon lord to Golarion.

name: Kostchtchie
title: The Deathless
description: Kostchtchie is the Demon Lord of cold and giant-kind. He embodies the destructive power of winter and the strength of frost giants. His followers are those who revel in destruction and ice.

name: Nocticula
title: The Queen of Assassins
description: Nocticula is a unique demon lord who serves neither good nor chaos, representing assassins and darkness. She was later redeemed and ascended to demigod status. Her followers are assassins and those who work in shadows.

name: Pazuzu
title: The King of the Sky
description: Pazuzu is the Demon Lord of the sky and corruption. He spreads plague and corruption through the air itself. His followers include air elementalists and those who seek to corrupt the world.

name: Shax
title: The Betrayer
description: Shax is the Demon Lord of murder, lies, and betrayal. He represents the chaos of violence and the power of deception. His followers are murderers and liars devoted to chaos.

name: Charon
title: The Boatman of the Dead
description: Charon is the Horseman of Death, ruler of the dark waters of the river Styx. He ferries the dead to judgment. He represents the inevitability of death and the final passage.

name: Szuriel
title: The Stained Harlot
description: Szuriel is the Horseman of War, embodying the chaos and suffering of endless conflict. She represents the meaningless carnage and suffering of warfare. Her followers revel in violence and battle.

name: Apollyon
title: The Plague Lord
description: Apollyon is the Horseman of Pestilence. He spreads disease and plague across the world. He represents the unstoppable nature of epidemic and decay.

name: Trelmarixian
title: The Silent Savant
description: Trelmarixian is the Horseman of Famine. She brings hunger and despair to the lands. She represents the suffering of starvation and the despair that follows want.

name: The Green Mother
title: The Mother
description: The Green Mother is one of the Eldest, powerful fey lords. She represents nature's hunger and the allure of the wild. She seduces mortals and feeds on those she lures to her domain.

name: The Lantern King
title: The Jester
description: The Lantern King is one of the most powerful Eldest, a capricious fey lord of mischief and transformation. His true nature remains mysterious, and he enjoys games and manipulation.

name: Ng
title: The Keeper of Secrets
description: Ng is one of the Eldest, representing the cycles of seasons and time. She keeps the secrets of ages and the turning of the world.

name: Ragadahn
title: The Dragon of the Depths
description: Ragadahn is one of the Eldest, taking the form of a powerful linnorm. He represents chaos and destruction in the depths of the ocean.

name: Shyka
title: The Many-Faced
description: Shyka is one of the most ancient Eldest, embodying entropy and change. She exists outside normal time and represents the inevitable decay and transformation of all things.

name: Count Ranalc
title: The Exile
description: Count Ranalc is one of the Eldest, representing betrayal and the plight of exiles. He dwells in shadows and teaches the ways of treachery and survival in darkness.

name: The Prophesies of Kalistrade
title: The Philosophy of Law
description: The Prophesies of Kalistrade represent a lawful philosophy without deity worship. Followers believe in self-discipline, morality through law, and prosperity through trade. This is practiced widely in Rahadoum and among merchants.

name: The Green Faith
title: Nature Itself
description: The Green Faith is a druidic philosophy worshipping nature itself rather than individual deities. Followers believe in the sacredness of the natural world and the balance of all living things. This faith is widespread among druids, rangers, and wilderness folk.

name: The Laws of Mortality
title: The Philosophy of Godlessness
description: The Laws of Mortality represent a philosophy of Rahadoum rejecting all divine worship. Followers believe in reason, mortality, and self-determination without divine intervention. This state religion of Rahadoum completely rejects organized religion.
